diagrams.title: Amdahl's Law vs Gustafson's Law Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison showing how Amdahl's Law (fixed problem size) plateaus while Gustafson's Law (scaled problem) continues to improve with processors
elements: Amdahl curve: Rapid initial rise then plateaus
Serial portion marked as ceiling on Amdahl's
Annotation: 'As problem scales, parallel portion grows' for Gustafson
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate why choosing appropriate problem sizes matters in parallel computing decisions for cloud workloads

diagrams.title: Three-Tier Architecture
diagrams.description: Classic three-tier cloud application architecture with presentation, application, and data tiers showing request flow
elements: Client devices (browser, mobile) → Load Balancer → Web Servers (Presentation) → Application Servers (Business Logic) → Database Cluster (Data)
Scalability annotations: 'Auto-scale based on load' for presentation, 'Stateless' for application
diagrams.purpose: Show component interaction and scalability points in typical cloud-deployed applications

diagrams.title: Pipeline Parallelism Timing Diagram
diagrams.description: Time-space diagram showing pipeline processing with fill, steady-state, and drain phases for 4 stages processing 6 items
elements: Rows: Time units 1-10, Columns: Stage 1, Stage 2, Stage 3, Stage 4
Show items A-F moving through stages
Highlight pipeline fill period (first 4 time units)
Highlight steady-state (items complete every 1 time unit)
diagrams.purpose: Illustrate how pipeline parallelism achieves throughput improvement despite increased latency for first item
